# geordfconverter
Converts relational datasets with or without a schema to RDF

## Mappingschema Definition

Each mappingschema definition requires the following attributes to be set:

* **id**: The ID column of the relational dataset. If no ID column is provided a heuristic tries to infer it. If that fails, an ascending ID will be generated.
* **datanamespace**: The namespace to use for the data instances in the mapped dataset. If non is provided, a fallback is used.
* **namespace**: The namespace used for vocabularies which are autogenerated from column names

### Classmappings

Each relational dataset maps to one or more RDF classes, out of which one class is determined the main class to focus on for the mapping conversion.

```
"class":{"uri":"http://archaeoinformatics.link/ontology#MilitaryCamp","labels":{"en":"Military Camp","de":"Militärcamp"}}
```

A main class definition consists of a URI defining the class as well as of a list of labels describing the class in various languages.
A definition of the class may be added using a "definition" JSON object.

Further classmappings can be added using a JSON array:

```
"classmappings":[
    {"uri":"https://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q88205","labels":{"en":"Castrum","de":"Kastell"}}
]
```

### Columnmappings

A column mapping maps a column of a relational dataset to a URI describing its contents and a possible datatype and are defined as a JSON object.

```
"columns":{
       "ID":{"prop":"obj","ignore":true},
       "limesobject": {"proplabels":{"en":"label","de":"Label"},"prefix":"Kleinkastell ","suffix":"!","propiri":"http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label","range": "xsd:string","prop":"anno", "order": 1},
       "limescategory": {"propiri":"http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf","prop":"subclass", "order": 2},
       "limestown":{"prop":"obj","valuemapping":{"Bad Homburg":"https://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q4165"}}
   }
```
Each column mapping consists of at least the following entries:

* **ignore**: If set to true, this column will be ignored for further processing. Use this flag for columns which are not relevant to RDF conversion.
* **order**: Gives an order to the properties in the mapping. This order may be saved in the RDF graph and used for Downlifting tasks
* **prefix**: Allows to add a prefix to the column value in the processing step, e.g. if the column value is "hello" and the prefix value is "hi", the processed result will be "hi hello"
* **prop**: with values "data","obj","anno","subclass" to indicate the role of the property with relation to the dataset as "owl:DatatypeProperty", "owl:ObjectProperty", "owl:AnnotationProperty" or as a "rdfs:subClassOf" relation
* **propiri**: A URI to use to describe the column, if known
* **proplabels**: A JSON object of additional labels for the given property which will be added to the result graph
* **range**: The range of the property, e.g. xsd:string, xsd:integer. XSD prefixes will be resolved by the converter tool
* **suffix**: Allows to add a suffix to the column value in the processing step, e.g. if the column value is "hello" and the suffix value is "hi", the processed result will be "hello hi"
* **valuemapping**: A mapping of column values to RDF concepts. The column value will be preserved as the label of the column concept

### Adding Additional columns

Additional columns which are not present in the dataset can be added using an addcolumns definition:

```
"addcolumns":{
        "limesobject": {"propiri":"http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#comment","value":"This is a generic comment","range": "xsd:string","prop":"anno"}
}
```
An addcolumns definition may contain the same attributes as a previously mentioned column defintion, but requires a value to be provided, since no actual column is available to process.

### Grouping Columns

Columns may be grouped by defining column collections.



## Automapping

The geordfconverter is able to infer a limited automatic mapping with the following contents:
* Columns as data properties with their inferred ranges
* A best guess for the ID property
